HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RACHEL RENDEL's Motion To Modify Subpoena Duces Tecum Of Rachel Rendel.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Plaintiff's motion is granted in part and denied in part. The requested documents are to be produced subject to the parties' stipulated protective order, and are to be produced by May 1, 2023.

Defense counsel is to prepare a proposed order, submit it to opposing counsel for approval as to the form, and then submit it to the pro tem judge at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Notice Of The Rendel Family Limited Partnership I And Rachel Rendel'S Motion To Modify Subpoenas Duces Tecum Issued To Ubs Financial Services, Inc., Gxii, Inc., Baker Tilly Us, Neuberger Berman Investment Advisors, Llc, And Charles Schwab Corporation
Off Calendar Sep-30-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Melinda Steuer, Esq. (916.930.0045; msteuer@californiainvestoradvocate.com) for Plaintiff.
Neal Robb, Esq. (562.436.2000; neal.robb@kyl.com) of Keesal, Young & Logan for Defendants.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RACHEL RENDEL, AS GENERAL PARTNER OF THE RENDEL FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP I'S Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint:

Plaintiff Rachel Rendel's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted.

"California courts have shown a liberal attitude toward allowing amendment of pleadings to avoid the harsh results imposed by statutes of limitations. Thus, proper amendments to an original complaint 'relate back' to the date of the filing of the original complaint, despite the amendments being made after the statute of limitations has expired. The policy behind statutes of limitations is to put defendants on notice of the need to defend against a claim in time to prepare a fair defense on the merits. This policy is satisfied when recovery under an amended complaint is sought on the same basic set of facts as the original pleading." (Garrison v. Board of Directors (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 1670, 1677-78 (citations omitted); see also Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial, Ch. 6-E, 6:736 ["It is the sameness of the facts rather than the rights or obligations arising from those facts that is determinative."].)

Plaintiff seeks leave to substitute the Rendel Family Limited Partnership I, a California limited partnership, for the current plaintiff, "Rachel Rendel, as General Partner of the Rendel Family Limited Partnership I, a California limited partnership." The proposed amended pleading does not state any new causes of action or add any new facts; it alleges the identical facts, communications, and harm regarding the identical investment account as the original complaint. As in Garrison, the proposed amended pleading states the "same basic set of facts as [the] original petition" and "[t]he change effected by the amendment is one of form, not of substance, and should be allowed in the interests of justice." (36 Cal.App.4th at 1678.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
